title: How Investors May Respond To Rush Enterprises (RUSH.A) Naming Jody Pollard COO Before Q1 2026 Earnings
content:
In March 2026, Rush Enterprises appointed longtime executive Jody Pollard as Chief Operating Officer, succeeding Jason Wilder, who left the company, with Pollard reporting to Chairman and CEO Rusty Rush and supported during the transition by senior adviser and former COO Michael McRoberts. Pollard’s extensive background across truck sales, aftermarket services and multi-location dealership operations gives him deep insight into Rush Enterprises’ core profit drivers at a time when the company is preparing to report its first-quarter 2026 earnings. With Pollard’s promotion to COO, we’ll examine how this leadership transition could influence Rush Enterprises’ existing investment narrative and execution focus.

Uncover the next big thing with 33 elite penny stocks that balance risk and reward.

Rush Enterprises Investment Narrative Recap

To own Rush Enterprises, you need to believe its nationwide dealership and aftermarket platform can keep generating solid, recurring earnings despite cyclical truck demand and regulatory uncertainty. The COO change looks more like continuity than disruption, given Jody Pollard’s long tenure in operations and truck/aftermarket sales, so it does not materially alter the near term focus on how Q1 2026 results reflect freight conditions and emissions or tariff risk.

The most relevant recent announcement is Rush’s upcoming first quarter 2026 earnings release and conference call on April 28–29, 2026. That update will sit alongside Pollard’s appointment and may give investors a clearer sense of how leadership is prioritizing aftermarket growth, truck sales mix and capital allocation in light of softer recent margins and the industry’s regulatory backdrop.

Yet while leadership looks stable, investors should still be aware of how prolonged freight softness could compound Rush’s exposure to cyclical truck demand and ...

Read the full narrative on Rush Enterprises (it's free!)

Rush Enterprises' narrative projects $8.6 billion revenue and $397.0 million earnings by 2029. This requires 5.0% yearly revenue growth and a $133.2 million earnings increase from $263.8 million today.

Uncover how Rush Enterprises' forecasts yield a $78.67 fair value, a 20% upside to its current price.

Exploring Other PerspectivesRUSH.A 1-Year Stock Price Chart

The most optimistic analysts were assuming revenue of about US$8.4 billion and earnings of roughly US$441.0 million by 2029, which leans heavily on aftermarket resilience and leasing growth. Compared with the baseline focus on freight and regulatory pressure, this more upbeat view could be challenged or reinforced by how Pollard steers parts, service and leasing after his promotion, so it is worth weighing both narratives before you decide which assumptions feel closer to your own.

title: Rush Enterprises, Inc. Conference Call Advisory for First Quarter 2026 Earnings Results
content:
Rush Enterprises, Inc.

NEW BRAUNFELS, Texas, April 02, 2026 (GLOBE NEWSWIRE) -- Rush Enterprises, Inc., (NASDAQ: RUSHA & RUSHB), which operates the largest network of commercial vehicle dealerships in North America will host a conference call to discuss earnings for the first quarter 2026 on Wednesday, April 29, 2026 at 10:00 a.m. Eastern/9:00 a.m. Central. Earnings will be reported after the close of market on Tuesday, April 28, 2026.

The call will be available at http://investor.rushenterprises.com/events.cfm on Wednesday, April 29, 2026 at 10:00 a.m. Eastern/9:00 a.m. Central.

Participants may register for the call at:
https://register-conf.media-server.com/register/BI31f424b7e9f24f34915b723b0fb189bd
While not required, it is recommended that you join the event 10 minutes prior to the start.

For those who cannot listen to the live broadcast, the webcast replay will be available at http://investor.rushenterprises.com/events.cfm.

About Rush Enterprises, Inc.
Rush Enterprises, Inc. is the premier solutions provider to the commercial vehicle industry. The Company owns and operates Rush Truck Centers, the largest network of commercial vehicle dealerships in North America, with more than 150 locations in 23 states and Ontario, Canada. These vehicle centers, strategically located in high traffic areas on or near major highways throughout the United States and Ontario, Canada, represent truck and bus manufacturers, including Peterbilt, International, Hino, Isuzu, Ford, IC Bus and Blue Bird. They offer an integrated approach to meeting customer needs – from sales of new and used vehicles to aftermarket parts, service and body shop operations plus financing, insurance, leasing and rental. Rush Enterprises' operations also provide CNG fuel systems (through its investment in Cummins Clean Fuel Technologies, Inc.), telematics products and other vehicle technologies, as well as vehicle up-fitting, chrome accessories and tires. For more information, please visit us at www.rushtruckcenters.com, www.rushenterprises.com and www.rushtruckcentersracing.com, on Twitter @rushtruckcenter and Facebook.com/rushtruckcenters.

Contact:
Rush Enterprises, Inc., New Braunfels, Texas
Steve Keller (830) 302-5226
